title: Cirsium tenoreanum
intro: Cirsium tenoreanum, also known as the Pyrenean thistle, is a perennial plant species within the sunflower family (Asteraceae) characterized by its prickly stems and purple flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its use in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents responsible for these effects include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic applications. In herbal medicine, the Pyrenean thistle is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and also used in the production of herbal capsules and supplements.
taxonomy: Cirsium tenoreanum is classified as a species of thistle in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Cirsium, which includes other thistle species. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships. The species is further classified as a member of the subgenus Cirsium subg. Cirsium, which includes other species with similar characteristics. The classification of Cirsium tenoreanum is an important aspect of botanical taxonomy.
